assertion_results.text: Step 1 extracts imprecise affected range from the Jira description — the description lacks a specific version threshold (Step 1 Data Extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, the output explicitly flags the imprecision: 'Affected version range | versions prior to the fix (imprecise -- no exact threshold in Jira description)' and 'Fixed version | see advisory (imprecise -- no exact version in Jira description)'. The Missing Data Flag section further states: 'The Jira description provides only imprecise version information: Affected versions: "versions prior to the fix" -- no semver threshold; Fixed version: "see advisory" -- no exact version'.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 queries MITRE CVE API and extracts lessThan 0.4.8 as the fix threshold from the affected[].versions[] field (§1.62)
assertion_results.evidence: In enrichment.md, the MITRE CVE API section states: 'Source: https://cveawg.mitre.org/api/cve/CVE-2026-48901' and 'Affected range: versions less than 0.4.8 (lessThan: "0.4.8", versionType: semver)' with 'Fix threshold: 0.4.8'.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 queries OSV.dev API and extracts fixed version 0.4.8 from the affected[].ranges[].events field (§1.62)
assertion_results.evidence: In enrichment.md, the OSV.dev API section states: 'Source: https://api.osv.dev/v1/vulns/CVE-2026-48901' and 'Introduced: 0 (all versions from origin)' and 'Fixed: 0.4.8'. The data matches the affected[].ranges[].events structure (introduced/fixed events).

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 cross-validation shows agreement between MITRE and OSV.dev — the enriched fix threshold (0.4.8) is used as the authoritative value for Step 2.3
assertion_results.evidence: In enrichment.md, the Cross-Validation Result section states: 'Agreement: Both MITRE CVE API and OSV.dev agree on the fix threshold of 0.4.8.' The Enriched Fix Threshold section confirms: 'Fix threshold: 0.4.8 (from cross-validated external sources)' with 'Confidence: High (two independent external sources agree)' and explicitly states: 'This enriched fix threshold (h2 >= 0.4.8 is NOT affected, h2 < 0.4.8 IS affected) will be used in Step 2.3 for version impact comparisons.'

assertion_results.text: Step 2.3 version impact comparison uses the enriched fix threshold (0.4.8) from Step 1.5, not the imprecise Jira description data
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Enriched Fix Threshold section explicitly states: 'From Step 1.5 cross-validation: h2 < 0.4.8 is affected.' All version comparisons in the impact table use 0.4.8 as the threshold (e.g., '0.4.5 < 0.4.8' for affected, '0.4.8 >= 0.4.8 (at fix threshold)' for not affected, '0.4.9 >= 0.4.8' for not affected). The imprecise Jira description data ('versions prior to the fix') is not used for comparisons.
